Basically, this looks like a fat, rounded heart. - Cut out template and draw onto foam sheets. - Cut foam lily pads. - With a marker, draw a fat number, from 1-10, onto each lily pad. Play: Play by numbers or use blank sides and play by color. Place lily pads in numerical order, if playing by number. These should be a distance apart that makes it easy for your preschooler to hop from one lily pad to the next. - Tell child to hop from Number 1 all the way to Number 10. If a child is just learning numbers, this is a great game to help learn numbers. Depending on the age of your child, younger children can hop on both feet and older children can hop on one foot, which will help develop good balance. - If using colors, set up lily pads in any color sequence but announce the colors to hop to for your child. Title: How to Convert a Photo to iPod Format Introduction: Converting photos to an iPod-compatible format is necessary if you want to view your pictures on your iPod Touch or iPhone. The default photo format for most digital cameras and computers is JPEG (or JPG), but Apple devices use a different standard called HEIC. This tutorial will guide you through converting your JPEGs into HEIC using various methods. Method 1 - Using macOS Photos App: If you're a Mac user, you can leverage the built-in Photos app to convert your images quickly. Step 1: Open Photos App Launch the Photos application from your Applications folder or Dock. If you haven't already imported your photos, do so now by clicking 'File' > 'Import'. Step 2: Select Images Choose the picture(s) you wish to convert by clicking on them while holding down the Command key ( ). Step 3: Export as HEIC Click 'File' > 'Export'> 'Export Unmodified Original For X Items', where X represents the number of selected images. A dialog box should appear; change the file format to 'HEVC & Alpha' before hitting 'Export.' Key Tips: * Keep the original copies of your files until you ensure that the conversion process has been successful. * Remember that some older iOS versions may not support HEVC format natively; consider converting back to JPEG if needed. Method 2 - Online Conversion Tools: For those who don't have access to a Mac or prefer web-based tools, online services like CloudConvert offer easy conversions between formats including JPG to HEIC. Step 1: Visit Website Navigate to www.cloudconvert.com within any modern web browser such as Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge etc. Step 2: Upload File Drag your JPEG image onto the dotted area marked 'Drop Files Here' or click inside this region to open up a file picker window. Step 3: Choose Output Format Once uploaded, under 'Output Format,' select 'image heic.' Optionally adjust other settings according to preference. Step 4: Start Conversion Hit the red 'Start Conversion' button at the bottom right corner. After completion, download links will be provided. Guidelines: * Be cautious when choosing an online tool; make sure they are reputable and secure. Check their privacy policy regarding data handling practices. * Due to size limitations imposed by free plans, large batches might require splitting into smaller groups or paid subscriptions. Method 3 - Third Party Software (Windows): While there isn't a native Windows solution, third-party software like CopyTrans HEIC can facilitate this task effectively. Step 1: Download Software Visit https://www.copytrans.net/heic/ and download CopyTrans HEIC for Windows. Install it following prompts. Step 2: Add Photos After installation, launch the program. Click 'Add Photos' then choose either single image selection or entire folders containing multiple images. Step 3: Save As HEIC Select all added items with Ctrl+A, then click 'Save Selected.' Choose destination directory and confirm saving. All chosen photographs will now exist as .HEIC files. Tips & Guidelines: * Only install trusted software from reliable sources. Research reviews and feedback about unfamiliar applications before proceeding. * Always keep backup copies of important documents and media files outside of the conversion process. Materials Needed: 1. Foundation or BB cream that matches your skin tone 2. Powder (translucent or same shade as your foundation) 3. Cream eyeshadow in white or ivory 4. Eyeshadow in light pink or beige 5. Eyeliner pencil in black or dark brown 6. False eyelashes or individual lashes (optional) 7. Mascara 8. Blush in soft rose or coral 9. Lip liner in a color close to your chosen lipstick 10. Lipstick in red or deep rosy hue 1 Go over and say one for the money two for the show... [looks at MOT after pointing in the book] What Bees Do at Home This story is designed to be used with Leader Resource 2, Beehive Poster in Activity 3. What Do Bees Do at Home? How do you think bees get into their hives? (Point to the opening in the hive on the poster.) That's right; the hive has an opening so the bees can go in and out. Last week we talked about doors to our homes that we use to go in and out. The bees' home has a door, also. Some homes have a room or rooms. Beehives have chambers. Someone made the homes we live in and the beehive had to be made, too. Some people build their own homes; some live in homes that have been made by other people, whose job it is to make homes. The bees make the beehive themselves by producing wax and building honeycombs. What are some of the things people do in their homes? (You will probably get most of the desired responses: "eat," "sleep," "be safe," "stay away from rain/snow/cold/heat." If not, you can ask leading questions. As participants offer responses, describe the bee version of each human activity they suggest.) Do you eat at home? Bees eat at home, too. Do bees eat with spoons, forks or other utensils? No. Can you show me how you think bees eat? They eat pollen, nectar, and honey. Some of the worker bees have the job of leaving the hive to find pollen and nectar. After finding it, they store it in sacs on their legs and fly back to the hive, where they share it with all the other bees. Have you ever gathered vegetables from a garden or picked berries from a bush? Let's all pretend we are gathering food. (Stand up and pretend to pick vegetables or berries.) Does someone in your home leave to gather food? Some people farm the land for their food. Others work at other jobs to earn money or goods that they trade for food. No matter how the food is collected, people and bees come home to share their food with their families, communities, or colonies. A colony is what you call a hive of bees. Some colonies have 60, 000 bees! That's a lot of bees to feed! Bees rest and sleep in their hive, much as you do at home. How do you think bees sleep? (Allow participants to show you how they think a bee might sleep.) Worker bees take care of the young bees and old bees in their hive. People sometimes take care of children and elderly family members in our homes, too. We need our homes for protection. Beehives give bees protection from rain and storms. The hive protects the bees when it is too cold or too hot outside. We use our homes to store the things we need to live: like food and clothing. Bees don't need clothes, but the hive keeps their food, like honey, close by, so they can reach it when they need it. Within the hive, bees have special jobs to do. The drones are male bees that fertilize some of the eggs. There are not too many drones in a hive. Each hive has more drones than queens because there is only one queen in each hive. The queen's job is to lay the eggs and she can lay thousands of eggs a day! The queen also spends time with the worker bees, which help take care of her. Most of the bees in a hive are worker bees. These are female bees and they do all of the other jobs in the hive. Worker bees feed and take care of the eggs and baby bees. They build the hive. They add on to the hive when it needs to grow, by producing the wax to build the walls of the hive. They keep the hive cool or warm, as needed. They protect the hive from invaders. They leave the hive to search for pollen and nectar from plants, bushes, and trees and bring it back to the hive as food for everyone. They are always working to keep the hive healthy. That's why people talk about being "as busy as a bee!" In our homes, we have jobs to do, too. Some people clean and repair the home. Some take care of the young. Some people bring home food and some cook. Our homes provide for many of our basic needs, just like the beehive does for the bees. Bees in a colony also work together to make their beehive a nice place to live. This work is made possible by the generosity of individual donors and congregations. Please consider making a donation today. What makes hurricane winds so strong? Hurricanes are very intense and powerful storms. They start out as thunderstorms in tropical areas and make their way to land. They gather power and speed as they travel across the ocean. Hurricanes take in warm, moist air from the surface of the ocean and release cooler air above. As the air rises, it creates storm clouds and rain. More moist air comes up from the surface of the ocean. This cycle of moving air and heat in the atmosphere starts the winds rotating. Winds at the surface rotate and increase speed. Strong winds at the top, where the air is released, keep circulating at the same speed. This keeps the hot air out of the center (the eye) of the hurricane and the moist air keeps rising. This is what gives the hurricane its shape. When the storm hits the coast, it can let loose very strong winds, as high as 180 miles per hour (300 kilometers per hour). Because um of some of a high number. Solstices and Equinoxes The solstice is an event in the Earth's orbit during which the tilt of the Earth's axis is pointed most directly towards or away from the Sun. The summer solstice for the northern hemisphere occurs within a few days of June 21 every year. It is on this day that the position of the Sun in the sky at noon is at its highest altitude of the year, and the position of the Sun at Sunrise and Sunset is farthest north for the year. The winter solstice is around December 21, marking the date on which the Sun is lowest in the sky at noon and rises and sets farthest south. The day of the summer solstice is the longest day of the year, and the day of the winter solstice is the shortest day of the year. The equinox is the time of year when the Sun appears in the sky at the intersection of the ecliptic and the celestial equator. Two such points exist: The vernal equinox (approx. March 21) marks the start of spring. The autumnal equinox (approx. Sept. 20) marks the start of autumn. At each of these points, the Sun rises due east and sets due west. we're going to go see some more stones. I don't know why you like him. What cha want some cookies or a cake? How will you represent your chosen data for maximum effect? This activity is designed to be worked on in a large group or as a whole class. For more information, please read the Teachers' Notes. Everyone in your group or class will need a sticky note. Write your name on it. You could even draw a picture of yourself on it too. You'll also need some large sheets of flipchart paper or you could work on the board or the floor. Draw two long lines on the paper/board/floor, something like this (you might ask an adult to do this for you): You're going to use your sticky notes and the lines to help find out the answers to some questions. Here are some questions to start you off: We would love to see photos of the bar charts you create and to know how you went about making them. Could you give a name to the lines in each case? You will have questions of your own as well. We'd like to hear about the questions you asked and to see the bar charts you made to help answer them. You could begin this activity using the children themselves to create a block graph. Pose a question for the class to answer. A good one to start with would be "how could we show how many girls and boys there are in our class?". Take ideas and if not already suggested, sit or stand the children in two lines. Which line is the longer? Ask another question, for example, "how could we show how many children have school dinners and how many don't?". Again after suggestions, sit or stand the children in two lines and talk about what they notice. Now give each child a sticky note and ask them to write their name on it and decorate it in some way, should they wish. How could we use the sticky notes to answer the same questions? Give learners time to have a think themselves, then talk to a partner, then share with the whole group (think - pair - share). Some may suggest sorting or grouping the sticky notes on opposite sides of the board, which you could try to begin with. The disadvantage of this method is that it's not so easy to compare the number of boys with the number of girls (unless of course there is a large difference between the two numbers!). Ask the class how the sticky notes could be arranged to make the counting easier. At this point, some may suggest lining the sticky notes up in two rows or two columns, just as they had been doing at the beginning of the activity. You could then superimpose the axes of the block graph as a way of framing the picture, and invite children to suggest labels for each axis. If your class is familiar with block graphs, you could draw the axes on the board right from the beginning. Having had a go at one block graph all together, you could encourage the children to answer the other questions posed in the problem. They will also have their own ideas. Bear in mind that learners may find it easier to display qualitative data (such as boy/girl as above, birthday months) as opposed to quantitative data (such as number of siblings, number of pets, height) against the number of children. This is because when showing quantitative data, labelling the axes can be more tricky. This might be the case particularly with continuous data (those that use a scale, such as height) rather than discrete data (such as number of pets). However, try not to discourage children from posing questions involving quantitative data. Instead, listen to their discussions as they work out what to do. You may be able to address the above issues to some extent in a plenary by asking the whole group which question they found most difficult to display and why. Expanding Ivory Soap ===================== Ivory soap is known for its unique property of expanding when heated due to its high air content. This experiment can be both fun and educational, demonstrating principles of chemistry and physics. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to expand Ivory soap: Materials Needed ---------------- * A bar of Ivory soap * A microwave-safe plate or dish * A microwave * Oven mitts or tongs * Optional: safety goggles Instructions ------------ ### Step 1: Preparation Put on oven mitts or grab some tongs to protect your hands from heat during this process. While not mandatory, consider using safety goggles to prevent any potential soapy residue from getting into your eyes. ### Step 2: Plate Setup Place the bar of Ivory soap onto a microwave-safe plate or dish. Make sure the surface area of the soap is exposed as much as possible. ### Step 3: Microwaving Set your microwave to medium power (around 50%). Higher powers may cause the soap to overheat too quickly, potentially leading to popping or burning. If you only have one setting, monitor the soap closely. Now, place the plate with the soap into the microwave. Heat the soap for about two minutes. During this time, the water molecules inside the soap will begin to vibrate rapidly due to the microwaves, turning into steam which causes the soap to expand. **Key Tip:** Keep an eye on the soap throughout the entire heating process. Every microwave is different; therefore, cooking times might vary slightly. The goal is to see the soap puff up without letting it burn or catch fire. ### Step 4: Observation Observe the transformation happening within the microwave through the window. You should notice the soap starting to inflate like a foamy cloud. It's fascinating to watch! ### Step 5: Cautionary Measures Post-Cooking Once the allotted time has passed, carefully remove the expanded soap from the microwave using the oven mitts or tongs. Be cautious - even though it looks harmless, the soap could still be quite hot. Allow the soap to cool down completely before touching it directly. Remember that although the exterior appears dry, there could be pockets of hot air trapped inside. ### Step 6: Enjoy Your Expanded Soap After cooling, enjoy your expanded Ivory soap! Kids love playing with these giant clouds of soap, making them perfect toys for bath time. Alternatively, break apart pieces of the expanded soap to create DIY body scrubbers or laundry stain removers. Cleanup ------- Since Ivory soap is water soluble, cleanup shouldn't pose many issues. Simply wipe off any remaining soap particles with a damp cloth, then discard used materials responsibly. Conclusion ---------- This simple yet intriguing activity allows individuals to learn about scientific concepts while having fun. By following these steps meticulously, everyone can safely experience the magic of expanding Ivory soap at home. Mercury(I) compounds are weak oxidizing agents and weak reducing agents. Most of them are colorless. They easily disproportionate to mercury(II) compounds and mercury metal. They react with oxygen in the air to make mercury(II) compounds. Many mercury(I) compounds do not dissolve in water. Mercury(I) chloride is one of the most common mercury(I) compounds. Mercury(II) compounds are strong oxidizing agents and very corrosive. Mercury(II) compounds are red, yellow, or colorless. Mercury(II) oxide and mercury(II) chloride are the most common mercury(II) compounds in the laboratory. Can you say, bye bye and thank you? Did Kirsten eat baby food or real food? Find the Median of Numbers: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide The median is an important statistical concept that represents the middle value in a dataset when arranged in ascending order. This guide will walk you through finding the median of numbers with clarity, detail, and practical examples. By following these steps, you'll be able to calculate medians accurately and confidently. ### Step 1: Organize your data Before calculating the median, ensure that the given set of numbers is organized from least to greatest (ascending order). Arranging numbers systematically simplifies the process and reduces errors during calculation. Here are two sets of unordered numbers as examples: Set A = {4, 9, 2, 7, 5} Set B = {30, 8, 6, 21, 15, 10, 40} To organize Set A, arrange them from smallest to largest: {2, 4, 5, 7, 9}. For Set B, do the same: {6, 8, 10, 15, 21, 30, 40}. ### Key tip When organizing negative numbers or decimals, maintain their original values while arranging them; don't alter or drop any digits. ### Step 2: Determine if the dataset has an odd or even number of elements An odd number of items in a dataset results in one clear middle value, which becomes the median. Conversely, an even number of entries leads to two middle values averaged out to obtain the median. In our example datasets: * Set A contains five integers (odd), so its median can be directly identified as the center value after sorting. * Set B consists of seven members (even); hence, we need to find both middle points and then compute the average. ### Guideline If dealing with large datasets, quickly determine whether they have an odd or even count by checking divisibility by two. If dividing leaves no remainder, the dataset is even; otherwise, it's odd. ### Step 3: Identify the position(s) of the median(s) For an odd-numbered dataset, locate the single central index n, where n equals half the total count plus one. Then, pinpoint this exact element within the sorted array. For an even-numbered dataset, identify the pair of indices m and m+1 centered around half the length of the dataset (round down). These represent the positions of the two middle values in the ordered sequence. Applying this rule to our examples: * Set A: Half of 5 + 1 yields 3, pointing at the third position containing the number '5'. Thus, the median of Set A is 5. * Set B: Dividing 7 by 2 gives us 3.5, meaning we should focus on the fourth and fifth positions holding the values '15' and '21', respectively. Their average (sum divided by 2) serves as the median: (15 + 21)/2 = 18. ### Key takeaway Remember that working with fractions like 3.5 signifies searching between two whole indices - always round down first! By understanding these three essential steps and applying them meticulously, you can efficiently calculate the median of various numerical datasets, ensuring accuracy and enhancing analytical skills. Happy analyzing! Look at Mary doing [look at what Mary is doing]. Introduce Vocabulary: Arthur's Baby (Brown) What to Do Select three Tier Two vocabulary words to teach your students. A list of suggested words appears below. Write the vocabulary words on the board or on chart paper. 1. Introduce the story. Today we are going to read a story entitled Arthur's Baby. 2. Introduce the three vocabulary words you have chosen. Before we read the story, I want to introduce some new words that we will come across. Please repeat each word after I say it. 3. Read the story. Let's read the story. Make sure to listen for today's vocabulary words and to think about how they are used in the story. If you hear a vocabulary word while I am reading, raise your hand. 4. Define key vocabulary words. See definitions below. Let's think about our vocabulary words. The word ______________ means ____________. Does anyone remember how this word was used in the text? Call on students to answer the question. Then refer to the text to show how the word was used in context. Repeat this process for each vocabulary word. Now let's practice what we've learned. Adorable means really cute and lovable. What's the word? Everyone thinks puppies are adorable. You probably think furry baby animals are adorable. I'm going to name some words. If you think the words mean cute and lovable, say adorable. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? Advice means something you tell someone when he or she needs help. What's the word? Adults love to give advice to children. If you needed to paint your house, you could ask someone who paints for advice on how to start. I'm going to name some people. If you think you'd ask the person for help with homework, say advice. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - A baby - Someone who speaks a different language - A parent - A teacher - A classmate Germs refers to the tiny invisible bugs that live all around us. What's the word? Some germs can make people sick. You should wash your hands a lot so you don't catch cold germs from other people. I'm going to name some activities. If you think doing the activity might spread tiny, invisible bugs, say germs. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - Sharing a fork - Eating without washing your hands - Wearing clean clothes - Taking a shower - Sneezing on a friend Squeal means to make a high screaming noise. What's the word? People on a roller coaster often squeal. When you're playing tag, you sometimes squeal from excitement. I'm going to name some places. If you think it's okay to make a high screaming noise in the place, say squeal. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - The movie theater' - The playground - The swimming pool - A birthday party - The library Triple means three times. What's the word? The boy charged triple his usual fee for mowing a lawn that was really long and weedy. If you get three scoops of ice cream, it's called a triple cone. I'm going to name some things. If you would like to get three of these things, say triple. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - Birthday presents - Bad grades - Sore throats For Advanced Students: If time permits, have students create more examples for the vocabulary words. For Struggling Students: For ELL Students: In order to help ELL students learn the words, it may be helpful to use realia and/or to teach cognates. He wasn't interested in the older ones. The most common biome in South Africa is the grassland, mostly on the Highveld. This is where grasses, low shrubs, and acacia trees, mostly camel-thorn and whitethorn are more common then plants. Plants become even more less common towards the northwest. This is because of low rainfall. There are many species of water-storing plants like aloes and euphorbias in the very hot and dry Namaqualand area. The grass and thorn savanna turns slowly into a bush savanna towards the north-east of the country, with more thick growth. There are many numbers of baobab trees in this area, near the northern end of Kruger National Park. Once there was a little girl called Lucy who wanted to sell something very special. She carefully wrapped up her knee in a box and took it outside. Once she got to the market place, Lucy could see the many different people gathered together and she felt shy. She began to shout, I m selling my knee! One of the people at the market was an old woman who had seen many things in her life. She approached Lucy and said, Do you want some help selling your knee? Lucy nodded and smiled. The old woman was very compassionate and kind and she soon found a buyer who was willing to pay a good amount of money for Lucy's knee. Lucy could not believe her luck! The old woman then said, You should always be brave and use your compassion when trying to sell something. You never know who might be in the market place and be willing to buy it. Lucy smiled and said, Thank you so much for your advice and for helping me to sell my knee. The old woman smiled back at her and said, It was my pleasure. The intensity of a sound is how much sound energy goes through a square meter in one second. Sound waves with higher amplitude (bigger vibration) have higher intensity. The intensity of a sound is higher closer to the sound source. Farther away, it's less intense. The inverse-square law shows how sound intensity becomes smaller, farther from the source. "Inverse square" says that when distance gets multiplied by a number, sound intensity gets divided by that number squared (the number times itself). Thus, twice the distance means a quarter the intensity. You don't know what a picnic is? What camp site are you going to be on? But when it was nighttime he jumped out of it. Title: Be Like Amu Hinamori from Shugo Chara Amu Hinamori is the main character of the popular manga and anime series "Shugo Chara!" She is a strong-willed, determined, and kind-hearted girl who learns to embrace her true self throughout the series. To be like Amu Hinamori, you'll need to focus on developing your confidence, inner strength, and compassion towards others. Here are some steps to help guide you through this transformation: ### Step 1: Embrace Your True Self One of the most significant aspects of Amu Hinamori's character development is learning to accept and love herself. At first, she hides behind a tough exterior as the "cool and aloof" transfer student, but eventually reveals her genuine personality. * Key Tip: Start by identifying any traits or characteristics that you may have been hiding due to fear of judgment. Gradually allow yourself to express these qualities more openly around friends and family. This will enable you to feel more comfortable with being yourself. ### Step 2: Develop Confidence Confidence plays an essential role in Amu's journey toward becoming her true self. It takes courage to stand up against adversaries, face challenges, and make amends when necessary. You can develop your own sense of confidence by following these guidelines: * Identify your strengths and weaknesses. Focusing on what makes you unique will boost your self-esteem while acknowledging areas where improvement is needed promotes growth. * Practice public speaking or performing arts activities such as joining theater groups or participating in debates. These skills encourage self-assurance and presence during social interactions. * Encourage positive thinking patterns. Replace negative thoughts with affirmative statements about yourself and your abilities. Over time, this mental shift influences how you view yourself and approach new experiences. ### Step 3: Show Kindness Towards Others Amu demonstrates kindness consistently throughout the series, especially when dealing with those who struggle socially. By cultivating empathy and understanding towards others, you too can channel Amu's caring nature: * Active listening is crucial for showing respect and care for someone else's feelings. When engaging in conversation, give undivided attention to the speaker and respond thoughtfully. * Offer support and encouragement to people facing difficulties. Even small gestures like offering words of comfort or lending a helping hand go a long way. * Avoid gossiping or spreading rumors about others. Maintain integrity by treating everyone fairly and promoting positivity within your community. ### Step 4: Find Balance Between Schoolwork & Personal Life Balancing academic responsibilities with personal interests is vital for maintaining overall wellbeing. Amu manages school life alongside her duties as a Guardian Character, showcasing the importance of prioritization and organization. Implement strategies like creating schedules, setting goals, and allocating time effectively to ensure success in various aspects of your life. ### Step 5: Pursue Passions and Interests Amu has several passions, including music and sports, which contribute significantly to her happiness. Discovering your own talents and pursuing them wholeheartedly allows you to experience joy and fulfillment just like Amu does. Try out different hobbies and extracurricular activities until you find something that resonates with you personally. By incorporating these steps into your daily routine, you can become more like Amu Hinamori - confident, kind-hearted, and passionate about embracing your true self. Remember, change doesn't happen overnight; patience and persistence are key components of this process. I am talking to Joseph, he's listening to every word, he's absorbing it all. [pushes box of toys closer to DEA] Ben and Sam were best friends. They liked to play outside and go on adventures. One day, they saw a kayak by the lake. It was red and shiny and looked like fun. "Wow, look at that kayak!" Ben said. "Can we try it?" "I don't know," Sam said. "It's not ours. Maybe someone left it here." "Or maybe they don't want it anymore," Ben said. "Let's go and see." They ran to the kayak and touched it. It was smooth and hard. They looked inside and saw two paddles. "Let's go for a ride," Ben said. "It will be fun." "But what if someone comes back and gets angry?" Sam said. "We should ask first." "Who are we going to ask?" Ben said. "There's no one here. Come on, don't be scared." Ben climbed into the kayak and grabbed a paddle. He waited for Sam to join him. Sam looked around. He felt bad. He knew it was wrong to take something that was not theirs. He wanted to have fun, but he also wanted to be good. "Ben, I don't think we should do this," Sam said. "It's not nice. Let's go and play something else." Ben frowned. He was annoyed. He wanted to go on the kayak. He thought Sam was being silly. "Fine, be a baby," Ben said. "I'm going without you." He pushed the kayak into the water and started to paddle. He felt happy and free. He didn't care about Sam. Sam watched Ben go. He felt sad and lonely. He wished Ben would listen to him. He hoped Ben would be safe. Soon, Ben was far away from the shore. He looked around and saw the lake was big and beautiful. He saw fish and birds and trees. He smiled and paddled faster. But then, he heard a loud voice. "Hey, you! Get out of my kayak!" Ben turned and saw a man on the shore. He was big and angry. He was waving his arms and shouting. Ben felt scared. He realized he had made a mistake. He had taken someone else's kayak. He had been bad. He tried to paddle back, but it was hard. The wind was blowing and the water was choppy. He felt tired and sorry. The man ran into the water and swam towards Ben. He reached the kayak and grabbed it. He pulled Ben out and dragged him to the shore. He was very mad. He yelled at Ben and asked him why he had taken his kayak. He said he had been generous and let other people use his kayak, but only if they asked first. He said Ben had been rude and selfish. Ben felt ashamed. He didn't know what to say. He wished he had listened to Sam. He wished he had been good. He looked at Sam, who was still on the shore. Sam looked at him with pity and concern. He walked over to Ben and the man. He said he was sorry for what Ben had done. He said they were friends and they had seen the kayak and wanted to try it. He said Ben had not listened to him and had gone without asking. He said he had tried to stop him and had stayed behind. He said they had learned their lesson and they would never do it again. He said they were sorry and they hoped the man would forgive them. The man looked at Sam and Ben. He saw they were young and scared. He saw they had made a mistake and they were sorry. He felt his anger fade. He said he was glad Sam had been honest and brave. He said he was glad Ben had come back and had apologized. He said he would forgive them, but only if they promised to be good and to respect other people's things. He said he would be generous and let them use his kayak, but only if they asked first and followed his rules. He said he would teach them how to paddle and how to be safe on the water. He said he hoped they would enjoy the kayak and the lake, but also learn to be good and kind. Sam and Ben nodded. They said they were sorry and they thanked the man. They said they would be good and they would respect his kayak. They said they would like to learn from him and have fun with him. They hugged each other and smiled. They felt happy and relieved. They had made a new friend and they had learned a valuable lesson. They had learned to be good and generous. [successfully attempts to stand a silver man up on the silver circle on the black base] [1w Playing with toy dolls on slide. Starts rummaging through box again.] Rambling Rabbits Make /a/'s Rationale: To become successful in reading, individuals must acknowledge that each letter has a particular corresponding sound. This lesson will assist children in identifying a, the phoneme represented by /a/. The students will learn to recognize /a/ in spoken words by relating the letter a, practice finding /a/ in words, writing the letter a, and identifying the sound in spoken words through a read-aloud book. Materials: Primary Paper Chart with Abby ate Aunt Ally s apples. Pre-made cards with: Do you hear /a/ in cat or dog? Do you hear /a/ in mouse or rat? Do you hear /a/ in hat or bow? Do you hear /a/ in rabbit or bunny? Do you hear /a/ in him or man? Do you hear /a/ in fat or skinny? Do you hear /a/ in cry or laugh? Do you hear /a/ in white or black? Do you hear /a/ in bathtub or shower? Do you hear /a/ in crab or fish? Assessment worksheets identifying pictures with /a/. (URL s below) 1. Say: Our written language is a hassle at times. The hard part is figuring out which letter makes each sound and the movement that your mouth makes for each letter. Today we are going to work on spotting the mouth move /a/. We will spell /a/ with a. a looks like a bunny, and /a/ sounds like a rabbit rambling on. 2. Let s pretend to talk excessively /a/, /a/, /a/, /a/ [making mouth movement with hands like one is talking and hopping like a rabbit.] Notice what position your mouth is in when you say /a/. When we say /a/, you open your mouth wide and your tongue stays down. You try it out! It is like you are running out of breath when you have been rambling on too much. 3. Let me show you how to find /a/ in the word rabbit. I m going to stretch rabbit out in super slow motion and listen for my rambling stream of sound. R-a-a-a-bit. Slower! R-r-r-a-a-a-a-b-i-t. You got it! I felt my mouth open and stay open after the /a/ sound and then I said the rest of the word, closing my mouth as doing so. I can feel my mouth make that ramble /a/ in rabbit. 4. Let s say a tongue twister (on chart). Abby ate Aunt Ally s apples. Everybody say it 3 times together. Now say it again, and this time, stretch /a/ out at the beginning of each word. Aaabby aaate Aaaunt Aaally s aaapples. Try it again and this time break off the word: /a/bby /a/te /a/unt /a/lly s /a/pples. 5. [Have students take out primary paper and pencil] we use a to spell /a/. Capital A looks like a rabbit s cage. Let s write the lowercase letter a. Start on the fence line, and make a loop, lift your hand and put it back on the fence line touching the top of your loop and make a line from there all the way down to the sidewalk staying on your loop s side. I want to see everyone s a. After I put a smile on it for just fine repeat that nine more times. 6. Call on students to answer and tell how they knew: Do you hear /a/ in cat or dog? Mouse or rat? Hat or bow? Cry or laugh? Crab or fish? Say: Let s see if you can spot the /a/ sound in some of these words. Clap if you hear /a/: cat, dog, cast, last, first, math, sit, sat, crab, fish. 7. Say: In this book we will see what will happen when a rabbit rambles on. Clap your hands every time you hear the /a/ sound. 8. Show CAT and model how to decide if it is cat or dog: The /a/ tells me to ramble on, /a/ so this word is c-a-a-a-t, cat. You try some MAT: mat or met? Fast: fist or fast? RABBIT: rabbit or ribbit? 9. For assessment, distribute the worksheets. Students are to complete the papers by 1) circling the correct word and writing it and 2) line up to correct /a/ picture. Bruce Murray. Sure thing! I'd be happy to help create an educational piece based on the given snippet, simplified for grade-school students. --- Have you ever played with a Slinky toy before? When you hold it by the top and let go, the bottom end moves down in a wave motion until it reaches the ground. Now imagine stretching out the Slinky so that its coils are further apart. As you let go of the top, the waves moving down the Slinky get smaller and closer together until they eventually disappear when the Slinky is completely straight. This is similar to what happens when we study sequences in mathematics - a list of numbers arranged in a certain order. In this case, we're looking at a special type of sequence called $$a\_n = frac\{(-1)^n\}\{3 sqrt\{n)\}$$ where \(n\) represents each number in the sequence starting from 1 and going up to infinity. The notation \((-1)^n\) means that the sign alternates between positive and negative as \(n\) increases. So our sequence looks like this: * a = +1/3 * a = -1/6 * a = +1/9 * a = -1/12 * ...and so on Now, here comes the fun part! How do we figure out what happens to these numbers as \(n\) gets larger and larger? Do they keep getting bigger or smaller? Or maybe they start bouncing around in no particular pattern? To answer this question, we can pick a few large values of \(n\), calculate the corresponding value of \(a\_n\), and then observe any patterns that emerge. For example, let's try calculating \(a\_n\) for \(n=100, 1000,\) and \(10,000:\) * a = (+1/3 100) +0.1 * a = (-1/3 1000) -0.01 * a = (+1/3 10000) +0.003 Interesting! Notice how the absolute values of the numbers seem to decrease as \(n\) grows. They may still alternate between positive and negative, but their size keeps shrinking more and more. This suggests that as we continue to increase \(n\), the values of \(a\_n\) will become infinitely small and essentially "disappear," just like the waves in our stretched-out Slinky. To put it another way, the limit of this sequence as \(n\) goes to infinity is zero, which means that as we take larger and larger values of \(n\), the corresponding value of \(a\_n\) becomes arbitrarily close to zero. And that's all there is to finding limits of sequences in grade school math! I already have one valentine card in my bag. [picks up yellow and white checked triangle magnet and sticks it to the yellow wand] Title: How to Change the Voice Directions Volume on Google Maps for iPhone Google Maps is a popular navigation app that provides voice-guided directions to help you reach your destination. If you find the volume of the voice directions too low or high, you can adjust it to suit your preference. This tutorial will guide you through the process of changing the voice directions volume on Google Maps for iPhone. Step 1: Open the Settings App The first step is to open the Settings app on your iPhone. The Settings app has a gray gear icon and is usually located on the home screen. Tapping on this icon will take you to the main settings menu where you can customize various aspects of your device's functionality. Key Tip: Make sure your iPhone isn't set to silent mode before proceeding with these steps. If it is, you won't be able to hear any sound changes made within apps like Google Maps. Step 2: Scroll Down and Select 'Google Maps' Once you are in the Settings app, scroll down until you see 'Google Maps'. It should be listed alphabetically among all other installed applications. By selecting 'Google Maps', you gain access to its individual settings, which include options related to location sharing, notification preferences, and audio controls. Step 3: Choose 'Navigation' After tapping on 'Google Maps', look for the 'Navigation' option under the 'Settings' section. Navigation refers to the turn-by-turn direction feature provided by Google Maps. Here, you can modify several parameters concerning the way Google Maps guides you during travel. Step 4: Adjust the 'Voice Level' Slider Under the 'Navigation' settings, you'll find an option labeled as 'Voice level'. This setting allows you to control the loudness of the spoken instructions given by Google Maps while navigating. Move the slider leftwards to decrease the volume or rightwards to increase it. You'll notice the change in decibel levels indicated next to the slider as you move it. Guideline: When adjusting the voice level, consider factors such as background noise levels and personal comfort when listening to verbal cues. Setting the volume appropriately ensures clear guidance without causing disturbance to others nearby or straining your ears. Step 5: Test Your New Settings Now that you have adjusted the voice directions volume, launch Google Maps and initiate navigation for a route. Whether real or simulated (using the 'Start' button), pay attention to whether the volume suits your needs better than before. Remember, if necessary, you can always revisit the previous steps to fine-tune the settings further based on your experience. By following these five simple yet detailed steps, you now know how to effectively change the voice directions volume on Google Maps for iPhone. Enjoy safer and more comfortable journeys using optimized auditory assistance tailored just for you! Lily and Tom were playing dress-up in their room. [helping Sarah to make the appropriate movements as she recites] Title: How to Check the Maximum RAM Capacity of Your Computer Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of checking your computer's maximum RAM capacity. Understanding this limit is crucial when considering upgrading your system memory. By following these steps, you can ensure that any potential upgrade falls within your computer's capabilities. Step 1: Determine Your System Type The first step is to identify whether your computer runs on a desktop or laptop platform. This differentiation matters because laptops often have more restrictive hardware limitations compared to desktops. Additionally, certain types of laptops may require specific modules due to size constraints. Key Tip: Always refer to your device manufacturer's manual or website for accurate information regarding hardware compatibility. Step 2: Identify Your Operating System (OS) Knowledge about your OS version provides essential context for determining compatible RAM configurations. For instance, Windows XP supports different RAM standards than its successors. Here are some ways to find out which OS you're using: - On Windows, open the Start menu > Settings > About. Look under 'System type.' - On macOS, click the Apple icon at top left > About This Mac > Overview tab. It should list your macOS version number. Step 3: Find Out Your Motherboard Model Your motherboard model determines what kind of RAM slots you have available and their capacities. You can usually locate this info in one of two places: - On Windows, press Win + R, type "msinfo32," then Enter. The 'System Manufacturer,' 'System Model,' and 'BaseBoard Manufacturer' fields provide relevant details. - On macOS, check the same place as Step 2 above; look for 'Model Identifier.' Step 4: Research Compatible Memory Types & Limits Now that you know your system components, research online to discover exactly what sort of RAM they support. A quick Google search with terms like "[Motherboard Make & Model] max RAM" typically yields reliable results. Be sure to note down both DDR standard (e.g., DDR4) and speed (e.g., 2666 MHz). Key Guideline: Never mix RAM sticks of varying speeds or types, even if technically possible. Stick to identical specs across all installed modules for optimal performance and stability. Step 5: Count Current Slots & Used Modules Return to Device Manager (Windows) or About This Mac (macOS), depending on your system, and count how many physical RAM slots exist and how many are occupied. Each used slot reduces your remaining upgrade options. Step 6: Calculate Potential Upgrades Subtract the number of filled slots from your total slot count to determine how many additional RAM modules you could install. Then, verify that your planned configuration does not exceed your motherboard's channel limitation - most modern systems allow dual-channel mode, doubling theoretical limits for pairs of identically sized and specced RAM sticks. Example: If you have four empty slots but your motherboard only supports dual-channel mode, aim for adding two matching sticks instead of filling every slot haphazardly. Conclusion: By carefully following these steps, you now possess enough knowledge to make informed decisions regarding RAM upgrades. Remember always to confirm compatibility before purchasing new hardware, avoid mixing dissimilar modules, and consider future expansion possibilities while staying within budgetary constraints. Happy computing! [stops Gal from trying to pull at the lamb] I think it's the doggie next door. 'Cause they don't know who the story lady is. Title: How to Reset Your Forgotten Hotmail Password Introduction: In today's digital world, remembering numerous passwords can be challenging. If you have forgotten your Hotmail (now Outlook) password, don't worry! This step-by-step guide will help you regain access to your account by walking you through the process of resetting your password. We'll provide clear instructions along with helpful tips and guidelines to ensure that the procedure is as straightforward as possible. Step 1: Navigate to the Microsoft Account Sign-in Page Visit <https://login.live.com/> using your preferred web browser. This page serves as the entry point for signing into various Microsoft services such as Hotmail, OneDrive, Skype, Xbox Live, and others. By starting here, we begin our journey towards resetting your Hotmail password. Step 2: Initiate the Password Recovery Process On the sign-in page, locate the "Can't access your account?" link below the 'Sign in' button. Clicking this link will redirect you to a new page where you can initiate the password recovery process. The purpose of this step is to inform Microsoft that you need assistance recovering or changing your password due to forgetfulness or security concerns. Key Tip: Always remember to check if any suspicious activity has occurred within your account before proceeding further. Contact Microsoft Support immediately if necessary. Step 3: Choose a Recovery Option Once redirected, select the option "I forgot my password" under the main heading. You will then see several options for verifying your identity. These include receiving a code via text message, an automated phone call, or email. Select one that best suits your needs based on availability and ease of access at the moment. Guideline: Make sure you have access to the chosen method since it will play a crucial role in confirming your identity during the password reset process. Step 4: Verify Your Identity Follow the prompts provided to verify your identity. Depending on which recovery option you chose earlier, you may receive a verification code either via SMS or voice call. Enter the received code accurately when prompted. Alternatively, if you selected the email option, look out for an email from Microsoft containing a confirmation link - click on it to continue. Verification confirms that you are indeed the rightful owner of the account associated with the requested recovery information. Step 5: Create a New Password After successfully validating your identity, you will reach the screen where you can create a new password. Follow these recommendations while setting up your new password: * Use a unique combination of letters (both uppercase and lowercase), numbers, and symbols. * Avoid dictionary words, personal details like birthdates, addresses, etc., and common phrases. * Aim for a lengthier password (minimum 12 characters). * Consider using a passphrase instead - they tend to be easier to remember yet hard to guess. Confirm your newly created password by entering it again in the designated field. Confirmation ensures there were no typos during creation. Step 6: Complete Additional Security Measures (Optional but Recommended) Microsoft might ask if you want to set additional security measures like two-factor authentication or trusted devices. While optional, implementing them adds another layer of protection against unauthorized access attempts. Conclusion: Congratulations! After completing these steps, you should now have successfully reset your Hotmail password. Remember to log out of all sessions across multiple devices after making changes to maintain optimal security levels. Keep practicing good password hygiene going forward; consider using a reputable password manager tool to store complex password combinations securely. oh, no there would be five weeks honey. Title: How to Hand Wash a Shirt Introduction: Hand washing is an effective way to clean delicate or heavily soiled garments that cannot withstand machine washing. This method ensures your clothes are treated gently while removing dirt and stains effectively. Here's our comprehensive guide on hand washing a shirt correctly. Things You'll Need: - Mild detergent (like Woolite or Dreft) - Two basins or sinks - Clean water - Soft cloth or towel - Clothesline or drying rack Step 1: Check the Label Always start by checking the care label inside your shirt. If it says "hand wash only," proceed with these steps; if not, consider using a gentle cycle on your washing machine instead. Pay attention to any specific instructions provided by the manufacturer. Step 2: Prepare Your Workspace Fill one basin or sink with cool water and add a few drops of mild detergent. Stir until suds form but avoid creating too many bubbles as they can be difficult to rinse out later. Fill the second basin with clear, lukewarm water for rinsing. Key Tip: Never use hot water because it could shrink or damage the fabric. Step 3: Pretreat Stains Before immersing the entire shirt, treat any visible stains. Apply a small amount of laundry pre-treatment solution directly onto the stain and gently rub it in with your fingers. Let it sit for about five minutes before moving on to the next step. Step 4: Soak the Shirt Submerge the entire shirt into the soapy water. Gently swirl it around, pressing down softly so the soap penetrates the material thoroughly. Avoid wringing or twisting the shirt, which can stretch or distort its shape. Guideline: Depending on how dirty the shirt is, you may choose to let it soak for up to 30 minutes. Step 5: Rinse Thoroughly Transfer the shirts from the soapy water to the rinse water. Again, swish it gently to remove all traces of soap. Repeat this process if necessary, changing the rinse water if it becomes cloudy. Key Tip: Squeeze out excess water carefully - don't twist or pull roughly on the fabric during this stage either. Step 6: Remove Excess Water Lay the shirt flat on a dry, absorbent surface like a bath towel. Roll the shirt and towel together, pressing firmly as you go, to help extract remaining moisture without damaging the fibers through wringing. Step 7: Dry Properly Hang the shirt on a clothesline or drying rack to air dry naturally. Position the hanger or rod across the bottom edge of the shirt to maintain its original length and prevent stretching. Once dried, smooth out any wrinkles with your hands before wearing or ironing if needed. Additional Tips: - Always handle wet clothing carefully to prevent tearing or stretching. - Don't mix colored items with whites when hand washing to avoid color transfer. - Consider adding a tablespoon of white vinegar to the final rinse water to neutralize odors and brighten colors. Conclusion: By following these simple yet crucial steps, you will successfully hand wash your shirt without causing harm or reducing its longevity. Remember, patience and gentleness are key when dealing with delicates! = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Soderstrom/Joe/000530.cha = = = On The Farm can you feel the duck? Minesweeper is a strategy game that comes with most Microsoft computer operating systems. It is available for many other operating systems and desktop environments as well. The object of the game is to clear a minefield of mines. By clicking on blocks, the player learns if the block contains a mine. If they do not see a mine, the game continues. If they clicked on a mine, the game is over. When the player clicks on a block, a number may appear. The number shows how many mines are next to this square. This helps the player find out where the mines are. That's why she had ta go around the block? [Chi still humming Sis joins and then Fat joins.] Okay so that is the end of the study. Find People on Instagram: A Comprehensive Guide ============================================== Introduction ------------ Instagram is one of the most popular social media platforms today, with over 1 billion monthly active users. It has become an essential tool for individuals, businesses, and influencers alike to connect, share content, and engage with their audience. This guide will walk you through the process of finding people on Instagram using various methods, providing in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines along the way. By following these steps, you'll be able to expand your network, discover new accounts, or reconnect with old friends. If you are not already logged in, enter your credentials to access your account. ### Step 2: Navigate to the Search Bar On the home screen of the app, look for the search bar at the top (magnifying glass icon). Once there was a grumpy bear. He was always sad and lonely. He had no one to talk to and no one to play with. One day, a letter arrived for the bear. It was from a wise old owl who wanted to help the bear. The letter told the bear to come to the old forest to be healed. The bear was excited! He said goodbye to his house and set off on his journey to the old forest. When he arrived, he found the old owl who told him that if he wanted to be healed, he must be gentle, kind and patient with the animals of the forest. The bear followed these instructions and soon he noticed that he felt better inside. He was healing! The bear stayed in the old forest for many days. He made friends with the other animals, played with them and had lots of fun. Eventually the bear was so happy, he decided it was time to go home. He said goodbye to the old owl, thanked him for the letter and left the old forest, fully healed! Gravity holds galaxies together against the general expansion of the universe. In effect, the expansion of the universe takes place between groups of galaxies, not inside those groups. Gravity holds the galaxy together. The same applies to groups and clusters of galaxies, such as our Local Group where the Milky Way is, and the Virgo Cluster, a collection of more than 1,000 (might even be 2,000) galaxies. The gravitation is produced by the matter and energy in a galaxy or group of galaxies. Michigan is one of the fifty states in the United States of America. It is the 11th largest state in the United States. It is made up of two peninsulas (connected by the Mackinac Bridge): the only state to be so. It borders the U.S. states of Wisconsin, Indiana, Ohio, Minnesota, and Illinois. Its borders with Minnesota and Illinois are only by water. It also borders Canada by water. It was the 26th state to join the union on January 26, 1837. In 1847, Michigan became the first U.S. state to abolish the death penalty. By bookmarking a site, you create a shortcut that allows you to visit the page with just one click. This tutorial will guide you through the process of bookmarking a website in RockMelt, providing in-depth explanations for each step along with helpful tips and guidelines. **Step 1: Open RockMelt** Start by launching RockMelt on your computer. If you haven't installed it yet, download and install it from the official website. Once installed, open the application to proceed. **Step 2: Navigate to the Website** In the address bar at the top of the RockMelt window, type in the URL (web address) of the website you want to bookmark. Press Enter or click the Go button next to the address bar to navigate to the site. Make sure the website has fully loaded before moving on to the next step. **Key Tips:** * Always double-check the spelling of the URL to ensure accurate navigation. * Remember that some websites may redirect you to their homepage even if you enter a specific page's URL. **Step 3: Locate the App Button** On the left side of the RockMelt interface, find the vertical list of app buttons. These are small square icons representing various applications such as Facebook, Twitter, Gmail, etc. Scroll down until you see a blank app button, which can be used to add new bookmarks. **Step 4: Add a New Bookmark** Click and drag the blank app button onto the Edge panel located on the right side of the RockMelt interface. Release the mouse button when you see a blue outline indicating where the new bookmark will be placed. A dialog box labeled 'Add Application' should appear. **Step 5: Configure Your Bookmark Settings** In the 'Add Application' dialog box, fill out the required fields as follows: * **Title**: Type in the name you would like to give this bookmark. It could be the actual title of the website or any other descriptive term you prefer. The title serves as a label for the bookmark, making it easier for you to identify later. * **URL**: Verify that the correct web address (URL) is displayed in this field. RockMelt automatically fills it out based on the current active tab. However, make sure there are no errors in the URL. * **Icon**: You can choose a custom icon for your bookmark using this dropdown menu. Select an image that represents the website well or simply stick with the default icon provided. Once all the information is filled out correctly, click the 'Add' button at the bottom of the dialog box to finalize creating the bookmark. **Step 6: Access Your Bookmarked Website** Now that you have successfully created a bookmark, you can quickly access the saved website anytime. To do so, look for the newly added app button on the Edge panel. Clicking this button will instantly load the corresponding website in a new browser tab. **Key Guidelines:** * Keep your Edge panel organized by arranging related bookmarks together. Simply click and drag them around within the panel to reposition. * Remove unnecessary bookmarks by clicking the 'X' button in the upper right corner of the app button. Confirm deletion when prompted. By following these steps and guidelines, you can efficiently manage your bookmarks collection in RockMelt while enjoying fast and convenient access to your favorite websites. Happy browsing! Title: How to Perfectly Execute an Angle Park in 8 Simple Steps Introduction: Angle parking is a method of parallel parking that involves pulling your vehicle into a space at an angle, rather than backing in or head-on. This technique can be particularly useful when spaces are tight or when you need to park quickly and efficiently. With practice, anyone can master this valuable driving skill. Here's our eight-step guide on how to perfectly execute an angle park every time! Step 1: Choose Your Spot Before attempting an angle park, identify a suitable spot with enough room for your vehicle. The ideal location should have a clear line of sight and minimal obstructions behind you, such as parked cars, poles, or other potential hazards. Aim for a gap between vehicles that measures approximately one and a half times the length of your car. Tip: Always activate your turn signal before maneuvering into position; this alerts other drivers of your intentions and enhances safety. Key Guideline: Never attempt an angle park if there isn't sufficient space for your vehicle - doing so may result in damage or accidents. Step 2: Position Correctly Approach the target area slowly while keeping both hands firmly on the steering wheel. As you near the spot, check mirrors and over your shoulder for approaching traffic from either direction. Align your vehicle so that its right side (if you're parking nose-in) or left side (for rear-in angles) lines up evenly with the back bumper of the adjacent car. Step 3: Begin Turning Once positioned correctly, start turning your wheels towards the curb or kerb (nose-in angled parks) or away from the curb (rear-in angled parks). Maintain a steady speed and continue to monitor surrounding traffic. Keep turning until you reach about a 45-degree angle relative to the roadway. Step 4: Straighten Out As soon as your front end clears the rear bumper of the adjoining vehicle, begin straightening out your wheels. Continue moving forward cautiously, ensuring that your vehicle remains centered within the available space. Stop once your passenger-side mirror aligns with the back bumper of the vehicle beside you (nose-in) or your driver-side mirror matches the same alignment (rear-in). Step 5: Adjustment Time At this point, assess whether any further adjustments are necessary. If needed, pull forward slightly or reverse gently to ensure proper placement within the marked boundaries. Remember, leaving ample room on either side of your vehicle will make exiting the space easier later. Step 6: Complete the Process If everything looks good, shift into park (or neutral for manual transmissions), engage the emergency brake, and turn off your engine. Don't forget to remove your keys from the ignition and take all valuables with you before locking up. Step 7: Double Check Just before departing, double-check your surroundings to confirm no cyclists, pedestrians, or other motorists pose immediate risks. Once confident that conditions remain safe, proceed with caution. Step 8: Leaving the Space To exit the angled park smoothly, reverse your initial entry steps. First, check mirrors and blind spots thoroughly for incoming traffic. Next, center yourself within the space and begin reversing slowly. When clear, gradually straighten your wheels and complete the turn onto the street. Conclusion: Practice makes perfect - especially when learning new driving techniques like angle parking. By following these simple steps consistently, you'll gain confidence and proficiency in no time. Happy motoring! I cannot ride my scooter in the snow. In many jurisdictions the Head of State does not take part in the day-to-day governance of the jurisdiction and takes a largely ceremonial role. This is the case in many Commonwealth nations where the Head of State, usually a Governor almost exclusively acts "on the advice" of the head of the Executive (e.g. the Prime Minister, First Minister or Premier). The primary legal role of the Head of State in these jurisdictions is to act as a check or balance against the Executive, as the Head of State has the rarely exercised power to dissolve the legislature, call elections and dismiss ministers. I usually don't like sugar in my coffee. Title: How to Express Love to a Spouse Introduction: Expressing love to your spouse is an essential part of maintaining a healthy and happy relationship. It's important to regularly show appreciation, affection, and commitment to strengthen your bond and keep the romance alive. This comprehensive guide will provide you with actionable steps, insightful tips, and crucial guidelines to help you express love to your spouse effectively. Step 1: Verbal Affirmation Key Tip: Regularly communicate your feelings using words that convey love, respect, and admiration. Explanation: Hearing kind and loving words can significantly impact your partner's emotional well-being and reinforce their self-esteem. Make it a habit to tell them how much they mean to you, compliment their achievements, and share your gratitude for their presence in your life. Use phrases like "I love you," "You look amazing today," or "Thank you for being there for me." Remember, sincerity matters more than frequency, so ensure your expressions are genuine and heartfelt. Step 2: Quality Time Together Key Guideline: Schedule regular one-on-one time to engage in meaningful activities without distractions. Explanation: Sharing experiences and creating memories together fosters deeper connections between partners. Plan date nights, weekend getaways, or simple at-home activities tailored to both of your interests. By dedicating focused time to your relationship, you demonstrate that your spouse is a priority in your life and create opportunities for open communication and shared enjoyment. Step 3: Small Acts of Kindness Key Tip: Perform small gestures daily to show your thoughtfulness and attentiveness towards your spouse's needs and preferences. Explanation: Simple actions such as making breakfast, leaving a sweet note, or offering a back massage can make a significant difference in conveying your love and care. These acts serve as constant reminders of your devotion and consideration, contributing to an overall sense of security and happiness within the relationship. Step 4: Active Listening Key Guideline: Engage fully when conversing with your spouse by actively listening and responding empathetically. Explanation: Pay attention to what your spouse says, ask questions to clarify their thoughts, and validate their emotions. Show interest in their stories, opinions, and concerns, which communicates respect and understanding. Demonstrating active listening also encourages honesty and trust, enabling both parties to feel heard and valued in the partnership. Step 5: Emotional Support It started off l like that butterfly. If the entire fruit is fleshy, except for maybe a thin skin, we call the fruit a berry. A berry might contain one seed or many. Grapes, avocados, and blueberries are berries. They all have a thin skin, but most of the fruit is fleshy. Though don't get confused by the name of fruits like Strawberries, when actually, are actually "not" berries, because the seeds are on the outside: on a real berry, the seed or seeds must be inside. Find Out Your Own Glitches Introduction: Glitching is an alternative approach to manipulate video games in order to discover hidden content or features that were unintentionally left by developers. This technique has been widely used by gaming enthusiasts to explore new possibilities within their favorite games. However, finding your own glitches can be challenging yet rewarding. This tutorial will guide you through the process of identifying glitches in a safe and responsible manner. Step 1: Choose Your Game Select a game that you are familiar with and have spent considerable time playing. Familiarity with the game's mechanics, levels, characters, and environment increases your chances of spotting anomalies. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Opt for single-player offline games as they are more prone to glitches compared to online multiplayer games. * Consider choosing a game with complex environments or physics as these elements often contribute to glitches. Step 2: Understand the Basics Before diving into glitch hunting, understand the fundamental concepts behind glitches. They usually occur due to coding errors, memory mismanagement, or hardware limitations. Being aware of these factors allows you to focus on potential problem areas. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Learn about common types of glitches such as soft locks, hard locks, texture bugs, clipping issues, etc. * Research the history of glitches in your chosen game; this may provide insights into where to look. Step 3: Experimentation Start exploring different aspects of the game deliberately. Try performing actions outside the intended design, like jumping from great heights, walking through walls, or combining abilities/items in unusual ways. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Document every action so you can replicate it later if necessary. * Be patient; some glitches might require specific sequences or timing. Step 4: Observe Irregularities Pay close attention to any irregular behavior during your experiments. This could include visual oddities (like missing textures), sound issues, character control malfunctions, or unexpected changes in the game world. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Don't dismiss minor inconsistencies; they could lead to major glitches. * Compare your observations with normal gameplay to ensure it's indeed a glitch. Step 5: Replication Once you've identified a possible glitch, attempt to recreate it consistently. This involves documenting the exact steps needed to trigger the glitch. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Create a checklist or write down the series of events leading to the glitch. * Test the replicability across multiple playthroughs or platforms (if applicable). Step 6: Reporting If you're confident you've discovered a legitimate glitch, consider reporting it to the relevant authorities - typically the game developer or publisher. Many companies appreciate community input towards improving their products. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Always report responsibly without causing harm to others or promoting cheating. * Provide clear instructions on how to reproduce the glitch. Conclusion: Finding your own glitches requires persistence, creativity, and keen observation skills. While not all findings result in groundbreaking revelations, the journey itself offers unique insights into the inner workings of video games. Remember to respect game rules and avoid exploiting glitches for personal gain. Happy glitch hunting! And the monkey would throw mangos down to the crocodile. Solar noon is when the sun appears the highest in the sky, compared to its positions during the rest of the day. It occurs when the Sun is exactly halfway between sunrise (dawn) and sunset. This is also the origin of the terms a.m. and p.m., "ante meridiem" and "post meridiem". At solar noon, the sun is due south seen from the Northern Hemisphere, and due north seen from the Southern Hemisphere. The sun is directly overhead at solar noon at the Equator on the equinoxes; at Tropic of (latitude 23 N) on the summer solstice in June; and at Tropic of Capricorn (23 S) on the winter solstice in December. The queen smiled and had a good day. Hello young learners! Today, let's talk about a fun and interesting concept in mathematics called "matrix decomposition." You might be wondering, what in the world is that? Well, imagine you have a big pile of blocks that you want to rearrange into smaller piles so that it's easier to handle. That's essentially what matrix decomposition does, but instead of blocks, we're working with numbers arranged in a grid or table format called matrices. Let me give you a real-life example to make things clearer. Imagine you've been asked to organize your toys, which include cars, dolls, balls, and building blocks. It would take forever to count each toy individually, especially since there are so many of them. But, if you group them by type - all the cars together, all the dolls together, etc., suddenly counting becomes a lot easier. This process of breaking down a larger problem into smaller, more manageable parts is similar to what mathematicians do when they decompose matrices. Now, why would someone want to break down a matrix into smaller parts? Great question! Sometimes, multiplying two matrices together can be time-consuming and difficult. By decomposing a matrix first, multiplication becomes faster and less complicated. In our block example, suppose you had to move those blocks around several times before organizing them properly. Repeating this over and over could get tiring fast! However, if you broke up the stack into smaller groups, moving and sorting them would become a breeze. The same principle applies to matrices; once broken down, computations involving matrices become a whole lot quicker and simpler. So next time you see a giant math problem staring back at you, remember my little story about the blocks. Perhaps breaking down the problem into smaller chunks will make solving it feel like child's play! yeah, I turned the raining off of the sprinkler. oh, that's nice kisses for the puppy. [Father discusses bacteria with Ross and Mark] Grinding a rail is a popular trick in skateboarding that involves riding along a rail or ledge with your skateboard while balancing on top of it. It requires balance, coordination, and practice. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to grind a rail: **Step 1: Gather Your Equipment** Before you start grinding rails, make sure you have all the necessary equipment. This includes a skateboard, helmet, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards. Safety gear is crucial when learning new tricks as they can help prevent injuries. **Step 2: Find a Suitable Rail** Look for a low and smooth rail without any sharp edges. The ideal height should be around waist level so you don't need to ollie too high. Make sure there are no obstacles nearby that could cause accidents during your attempt. **Step 3: Practice Approaching the Rail** Start by practicing approaching the rail at an angle. You want to approach it slowly but confidently. Keep your body loose and ready to adjust if needed. Try different angles until you find one that feels most comfortable. **Step 4: Perfect Your Ollies** Being able to do good ollies consistently is essential for grinding rails. Spend time mastering this basic skill before moving onto more advanced ones like grinding. Remember to bend your knees, snap your tail downward, then slide your front foot forward to level out the board in mid-air. **Step 5: Position Your Feet Correctly** For grinding, place your back foot close to the tail and your front foot near the middle of the deck. This position allows better control over the board and makes it easier to balance while grinding. **Step 6: Approach the Rail Again** Once you feel comfortable with your setup, try approaching the rail again. As you get closer, prepare yourself mentally for the upcoming grind. **Step 7: Commit to the Grind** As soon as you reach the rail, commit fully to the grind. Jump slightly off the ground, leveling out your board, and aim to land directly on the rail. **Step 8: Balance While Grinding** While grinding, keep your weight centered over the trucks. Bend your knees slightly to absorb any shocks from uneven surfaces. Maintain a firm grip on the board with both feet. **Step 9: Exit the Grind Safely** To exit the grind, lean backwards slightly to lift the back end of the board off the rail first, followed by the front. Then, ride away smoothly. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Always wear safety gear when attempting new tricks. * Start small - begin with lower, smoother rails until you gain confidence and skill. * Practice your ollies regularly to improve your takeoff. * When landing on the rail, aim for the center where it's usually flattest and least likely to wobble. * Stay relaxed and focused throughout the process. Tensing up will only increase the chances of falling off. * Don't rush into things. Take your time to familiarize yourself with each stage before progressing to the next. Remember, patience and persistence are key when learning how to grind a rail. With consistent practice, you'll eventually get the hang of it! And the little boy is peeking out... If we don't have anything going on. Well you'll see Eve in just a minute. Here is an example of exploitation of labor. Jane is a shoemaker. She works for Michael, who owns a shoe factory that can make 60 pairs of shoes in a day. Jane makes 60 pairs of shoes every day. Michael pays Jane $20 a day. However, Michael sells each pair of shoes for $2 each. This means he makes $120 in a day. After he pays Jane her $20 wage, Michael has $100 left over. However he then has to pay for materials which cost $1 for each pair, so that's $60 each day. Then running expenses of the factory cost him $10 a day. So he only gets $30 at the end of the day for managing the business. This remaining wealth is called "Profit" or "Surplus [extra] Value." In other words, even though Jane makes 60 shoes every day, she only gets paid the value of 10 pairs of shoes. The rest of the day, while she is making the other 50 shoes, she is creating money for her boss. Her labor is making him richer and helping him earn money.
